howItWorks.intro: This ai job replacement timeline calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Risk Score = Base Field Risk × Task Type Multiplier - Experience Protection
Lifetime Income at Risk = Annual Income × Years to Retirement × Risk % × 0.5 (partial displacement)
Experience Protection = min(15%, Years of Experience × 0.5%)
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: Will AI replace my job entirely?
faq.answer: Most research suggests AI will transform jobs more than eliminate them. McKinsey estimates about 30% of work hours could be automated by 2030, but most workers will see their roles change rather than disappear entirely. Jobs combining routine tasks with judgment, creativity, or physical work are hardest to fully automate.

faq.question: Which jobs are most at risk from AI?
faq.answer: Data entry, basic coding, customer service chat, document review, translation, content summarization, bookkeeping, and routine analysis face the highest near-term risk. Jobs requiring physical dexterity (trades, surgery), deep relationships (therapy, sales), or novel creativity face lower risk.

faq.question: How can I make my career more AI-resistant?
faq.answer: Focus on skills AI struggles with: complex problem-solving, emotional intelligence, physical craftsmanship, ethical judgment, leadership, and creative strategy. Learn to use AI tools as a productivity multiplier rather than competing against them.

faq.question: What does AI-augmented work look like?
faq.answer: AI-augmented workers use AI to handle routine aspects of their job while focusing on higher-value tasks. A lawyer using AI for document review but focusing on strategy. A designer using AI for drafts but refining for quality. These workers are often more productive and valuable.
